title: Fragment: 'O Thou Immortal Deity'.
content:
O thou immortal deity
    Whose throne is in the depth of human thought,
    I do adjure thy power and thee
    By all that man may be, by all that he is not,
    By all that he has been and yet must be!
meaning:
Percy Bysshe Shelley's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Fragment: 'O Thou Immortal Deity'."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
